title: BCD and Excess Representations
summary: Explains BCD (Binary Coded Decimal) representation and addition algorithm, and the Excess-k representation with its representable interval.
content_points: BCD: each decimal digit stored in 4 bits; used in business/display applications.
BCD addition: if digit sum > 9 or carry occurs, add 6 (0110) to get correct BCD result.
Excess-k on n bits: value = binary_value - k; range [-k, 2^n - k - 1].
Excess-127 is used for the exponent field in IEEE 754 single precision.
learning_objectives: Describe excess-k representation and determine its range
